parityMeta.describes: iOS-17e-4 setWeightFineTuneEngine parity — steep e1RM climb tripping the noisy_trend fallback + the median trim + the UPPER projection clamp + the per-set filters. Four in-window push-a/bench-press sessions climb 50→80kg x 6 reps so |weeklySlope| > currentE1rm*0.1 (fallbackReason 'noisy_trend'); the top sample lands outside median*[0.7,1.3] so the trim drops it (effective = trimmed). Two sets in a daysAgo-10 session (rir 5; reps 30) and a daysAgo-400 out-of-window session carry huge loads that MUST be filtered — if any leaked, current/slope would change, so the golden pins the rir>4 / reps-out-of-range / window exclusions. FUNCTION-LEVEL compute-assert on the Swift side.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: Steep climb => |slope| > 0.1c => noisy_trend; top sample > median*1.3 => trimmed; projected > 1.04c => upper clamp. Excluded: rir 5, reps 30 (daysAgo 10), and out-of-window (daysAgo 400).
